A TINTIN-STYLE TRAVELOGUE

OJ's Grand
Indian Adventure

In which our intrepid European hero traverses the magnificent chaos of India, survives three Delhi Bellies, and discovers that the best adventures are the ones shared with a friend.

MUMBAI →
KOLKATA →
JAIPUR →
SHAHPURA →

MUMBAI →
INDONESIA
19 DAYS 4 CITIES 3 DELHI BELLIES 2 BEST FRIENDS
↓ scroll down to begin the adventure ↓
CHAPTER ONE

Mumbai

FEBRUARY 1 — 6, 2026
Where it all began

Our hero, the intrepid Olivier Jager — known to friends simply as OJ — touched down in Mumbai on a warm February evening with nothing but a suitcase, an appetite for adventure, and a stomach that had absolutely no idea what was coming.

His faithful companion Ram was waiting. The two had hatched this plan over months of WhatsApp messages: nineteen days across India. "How hard can it be?" OJ had asked. Ram had smiled the smile of a man who knew exactly how hard it would be.

Day 3. The pact was sealed in single malt. Mumbai, they agreed, was magnificent.

OJ settled into a charming Airbnb in Colaba, right next to the legendary Taj Mahal Hotel. His host Kozem turned out to be the kind of man who knew every hidden corner of the city — and promptly took it upon himself to show OJ all of them.

The days unfolded in a glorious blur: heritage walks across South Mumbai's colonial grandeur, badminton at the Radio Club (where OJ's competitive streak surprised absolutely no one), and long evenings discovering the city through Ram's eyes.

OJ, after his first auto-rickshaw ride "I have just experienced something between Formula 1 and a near-death experience. When can we do it again?"

One evening, Ram arranged a dinner at the French-Italian restaurant at Leela Kempinski — the kind of place where the napkins are folded into origami and the wine list weighs more than the furniture. OJ was joined by Ram's wife Shobhana and their daughters Ahilya and Anagha for an evening of warmth, laughter, and impeccable pasta.

Leela Kempinski. French-Italian cuisine. Indian warmth. OJ's stomach: still blissfully unaware.
And then, as is tradition for all European adventurers in India, it happened. The first Delhi Belly. Brutal. Unforgiving. A rite of passage that no guidebook prepares you for. OJ spent a night that we shall, out of respect, leave to the imagination.
OJ'S STOMACH STATUS
CHAPTER TWO

Kolkata

FEBRUARY 7 — 8, 2026
The soul

From the glittering chaos of Mumbai, our heroes flew east to Kolkata — a city that moves to an entirely different rhythm. If Mumbai is a Bollywood action sequence, Kolkata is a poet reading by candlelight.

Here, OJ was introduced to the world of KarmYog — and to Sourabh and Reena, whose warmth and vision for transforming rural India left a deep impression on our European traveller.

The KarmYog crew. Where turban meets vision meets friendship.

But the moment that truly shook OJ came in Uluberia — a small village where he was welcomed as a guest of honour. Walking into villagers' homes, seeing the construction of new community spaces, understanding firsthand how grassroots change actually happens — not in boardrooms, but in the dirt, brick by brick.

Uluberia. Building India, one basket of rubble at a time. No PowerPoint required.
At IIT Kharagpur's Research Park, OJ leaned forward in his chair as he learned how AI and the Vatika initiative were being deployed to transform rural India. For a man passionate about technology and its power to change lives, this was the kind of future he wanted to believe in.
CHAPTER THREE

Jaipur

FEBRUARY 9 — 12, 2026
The feast & the fury

On the 9th of February, Ram and OJ boarded a train to the Pink City for the wedding of Ram's friend RK. For OJ, an Indian wedding was exactly like being inside a fireworks factory during an earthquake — overwhelming, beautiful, loud, colourful, and utterly unforgettable.

OJ met Ram's college friends and was immediately adopted into the group. He danced — enthusiastically if not elegantly — and discovered that Indian hospitality has no upper limit.

The Jaipur Flower Market. Two grown men garlanded like visiting maharajas. Cost: 50 rupees. Feeling: priceless.
Ram, at Paro Restaurant, with dangerous ambition "OJ, you must try the Mutton Rogan Josh. Trust me."
Reader, he trusted him. What followed — at Paro Restaurant on the evening of February 11th — was an event that would be spoken of in hushed tones for years to come. The Rogan Josh was magnificent. OJ's stomach, however, had other opinions. Delhi Belly, Round Two.
OJ'S STOMACH STATUS — POST ROGAN JOSH

Undeterred — because true adventurers do not let a rogue lamb curry defeat them — OJ rallied. The following days brought a heritage walk across Jaipur that would fill a museum catalogue: the Govardhan Temple, the Kalki Temple, and then a visit to a home connected with the royal family that was so warm and inviting, it felt less like a tourist stop and more like being welcomed into someone's living room.

Tea with the royals. On the wall: ancestors who conquered kingdoms. On the table: biscuits.
Blessed with tilak. OJ: officially adopted into Jaipur royalty.
The Peacock Gate. 300 years of craftsmanship. OJ's pose: 3 seconds of preparation.

The next morning brought the flower market in full bloom — marigolds by the truckload — followed by a hands-on lesson in traditional sweet-making (OJ's technique was described politely as "enthusiastic") and then the grand finale: the magnificent Amer Fort, rising from the Rajasthani hills like something from an adventure novel.

Traditional crafts workshop. Ram: surgical precision. OJ: wrestling an octopus.
Kalki Temple. OJ contemplates the universe. Or possibly lunch.
CHAPTER FOUR

Shahpura Bagh

FEBRUARY 13 — 15, 2026
The oasis

After the magnificent chaos of Jaipur, OJ arrived at Shahpura Bagh — and the world went quiet.

Shahpura Bagh is the kind of place that makes you question your entire life choices up to this point. A heritage property set among ancient trees, with arched verandas, manicured gardens, floating marigold bowls, and the kind of silence that city-dwellers have forgotten exists.

Five arches. One sunset. Zero Wi-Fi needed.
Shahpura Bagh. Where OJ finally stopped running and the world stopped chasing.

Mornings began with breakfast on the veranda — pomegranate seeds, fresh coconut, chai, and the sound of absolutely nothing. OJ discovered that India isn't only magnificent chaos; it's also magnificent stillness.

Breakfast at Shahpura. Pomegranate, coconut, chai, and peace.
A bowl of floating marigolds. No caption needed. But here's one anyway.
Dawn at Shahpura. The road ahead has no Google Maps. It doesn't need one.

One morning, OJ visited a local school near Shahpura Bagh. On the chalkboard of Classroom No. 2, written in careful chalk handwriting: "We love our country. We respect our constitution. Jai Hind." No PowerPoint. No pitch deck. Just a chalkboard and the most powerful message OJ would encounter on the entire trip.

Classroom No. 2, near Shahpura Bagh. Zero slides. Maximum impact.
OJ'S STOMACH STATUS — IN RECOVERY
CHAPTER FIVE

Rajbagh

FEBRUARY 16 — 18, 2026
The calm before the storm (the stomach storm)

From Shahpura Bagh, our hero retreated deeper into the Rajasthani countryside to Rajbagh — a wellness and Ayurveda sanctuary where the only thing on the agenda was healing.

After weeks of spice warfare, OJ's stomach was finally granted a ceasefire. Ayurvedic oils were applied. Mantras were chanted. OJ lay on a massage table and experienced something he hadn't felt since landing in India: absolute, complete calm.

OJ, emerging from an Ayurvedic treatment "I think my soul just got rebooted."
For two days, time moved differently at Rajbagh. There were no auto-rickshaws, no Rogan Josh, no chalkboards. Just the slow repair of a body that had been through the magnificent wringer of India — and loved every minute of it.
OJ'S STOMACH STATUS — AYURVEDA MODE
CHAPTER SIX

The Finale

FEBRUARY 18 — 19, 2026
Mumbai, one last time

OJ returned to Mumbai for one final evening. A farewell dinner at The Fable — beautiful, elegant, emotional. Two friends who had just shared nineteen days of the most intense, beautiful, chaotic, stomach-destroying adventure imaginable, raising their glasses one last time.

The avocado starter at The Fable "You thought we were done? Think again, mon ami."
Because of course — of course — the avocado starters at The Fable almost did him in. Delhi Belly, the trilogy. OJ's stomach had one last battle to fight before departure. Some say the avocado was innocent. Ram maintains a diplomatic silence on the matter.
OJ'S STOMACH STATUS — THE AVOCADO INCIDENT
EPILOGUE

Indonesia

FEBRUARY 19 ONWARDS
The next chapter

And so, with a suitcase covered in invisible stickers — Mumbai, Kolkata, Jaipur, Shahpura, Rajbagh — and a stomach that had earned its battle scars, our hero boarded a flight to Jakarta.

From there, he disappeared into the remote islands of Indonesia, where turquoise water replaced terracotta walls, and the only danger was of the toothy, finned variety.

After 19 days of India's beautiful chaos, OJ found silence. And sharks. Mostly sharks.

But somewhere between the coral reefs and the tropical sunsets, one imagines OJ thinking about a chalkboard in a school near Shahpura, a bowl of floating marigolds, a sunset through five ancient arches, and a friend who made him eat Rogan Josh twice.

Some adventures end. This one just changed location.

OJ'S STOMACH STATUS — CAUTIOUSLY OPTIMISTIC
"The real voyage of discovery consists not in seeking new landscapes, but in having new eyes."
— Marcel Proust
CRAFTED WITH LOVE BY RAM · FOR OLIVIER JAGER · FEBRUARY 2026 · EDITION 1 OF 1
